text:
I know right now feels really discouraging, and that's completely valid. Failing an important exam is tough, and it's okay to feel disappointed. But I want you to know that this single moment does not define you or your abilities.

Some of the most successful people you know have failed exams, failed classes, and failed at things that mattered to them. What made the difference wasn't that they never stumbled—it was that they got back up. This exam doesn't measure your intelligence or your worth. It's one test, on one day, when you were dealing with your own unique set of circumstances. That's all it is.

The fact that this exam mattered to you means you care about doing well, and that's actually a strength. That same determination and commitment that made you want to succeed can now drive you forward. You've already learned something valuable from this experience, even if it doesn't feel that way yet. You know where you struggled, what you need to focus on, and what you're capable of pushing through.

Take some time to process this if you need to. Be kind to yourself. But then, when you're ready, you can look at what comes next. Whether that's retaking the exam, adjusting your approach, or finding support—you have options, and you have the ability to move forward.

This is a setback, not an ending. You've overcome difficult things before, and you can do it again.
